title: Earthy Russian Lentils Stew
description: A hearty, comforting stew featuring lentils, lardons, and a rich, earthy flavor profile.
instructions: Heat 2 tablespoons of oil in a large Dutch oven over medium heat.
Add the lardons and cook, stirring occasionally, until browned and crispy, about 8 minutes.
Add the onion, carrots, and celery and cook, stirring occasionally, until the vegetables are tender, about 8 minutes.
Add the mushrooms and cook, stirring occasionally, until they release their liquid and start to brown, about 5 minutes.
Add the white wine and cook, stirring to deglaze the pan, until the liquid is almost completely reduced, about 3 minutes.
Add the broth, tomato paste, thyme, salt, and pepper, and stir to combine.
Add the lentils, bay leaves, and water, and bring the mixture to a boil.
Reduce the heat to low and simmer, covered, until the lentils are tender, about 30 minutes.
Remove the bay leaves and stir in the red wine vinegar, if using.
Season the stew with additional salt and pepper, if needed.
Serve hot, garnished with fresh herbs, if desired.
Let the stew rest for at least 30 minutes before serving to allow the flavors to meld together.
